steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank sits after “Originally” and before “in ancient Greek,” and this whole phrase comes before a comma and an independent clause (“it has since been translated into other languages”). The opening phrase must be a participial phrase—a structure that can open a sentence and introduce a main clause.

steps.title: Apply the participial phrase rule
steps.content: The word “written” is a past participle—the -ed form of a verb used as a modifier—which creates an opening phrase that properly introduces the independent clause. The wrong choices (A, B, D) all add helping verbs (“is written,” “had been written,” “was written”), turning them into functioning verbs that cannot properly fill an opening phrase before the main clause. That matches choice C — C is correct.
